judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with precise file/line citations; they are comparable, with Report 1 marginally sharper on the shadow-sink removal semantics and Report 2 adding extra pipeline gaps.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:6` — `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"`**: because of the code path just described, leaving this set means **production patient/scheduling records get written into a staging S3 bucket** regardless of the `false` above. ... Delete the line.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:6` `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"`** — must be emptied or repointed to a prod-owned bucket.

report_1.evidence: the production frontend, as configured, would ship a bundle whose baked-in API base URL points at `https://staging-api.example.com`. ... Prod needs its own `.env.production` (with the real prod API URL) and a separate prod-mode build

report_2.evidence: the production frontend bundle ships with `https://staging-api.example.com` baked in.** For a real launch the pipeline must build with a production mode/env file (e.g. add `.env.production` with the prod API URL and build `--mode production`), and because one shared image is reused across environments, the API URL cannot be corrected at deploy time

report_1.evidence: `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but the runbook is "last updated: 3 months ago" and **nothing in the pipeline runs migrations** ... the fresh prod DB likely needs a manual first migration pass ... This must be confirmed, not assumed from the stale runbook.

report_2.evidence: `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but nothing in `deploy.yml` runs migrations, and the script that supposedly would (`scripts/deploy.sh`) is missing, so I cannot verify the schema (`backend/migrations/versions/0001..0003`) ever gets created on the fresh prod DB.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` was copied verbatim and *still* sets `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`. But the `deploy-prod` job runs with `environment: production` ... change `infra/prod/main.tf:3` to `environment:production`

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` **still** has `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` (copied from staging ...). But the `deploy-prod` job runs with `environment: production` ... It must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.
